content: If we treat long-term personal habit formation as a "self-refining large language model for behavior", we can borrow the stacked iterative feedback logic of Self-Refine and the dynamic persistent memory mechanism of Reflexion to build a fully unsupervised personal habit optimization system that does not require external coaching or supervised habit-tracking data. This system lets an individual iteratively critique their own daily behavior outputs, store successful self-correction patterns in long-term dynamic memory, and gradually refine bad habits over multiple cycles.
